Records.description: The ladder box integrals are considered with massive external legs and no internal masses. These are finite, and their value in 4 dimensions is expressed explicitly in terms of single-valued multiple polylogarithms.

4-mass ladder family, ladder4pt_L3_massless-internal-offshell-external. Planar L-rung box ladder: rails t_1..t_{L+1}, b_1..b_{L+1}; rail edges (t_i,t_{i+1}), (b_i,b_{i+1}); rungs (t_i,b_i); 4 off-shell external legs at t_1, t_{L+1}, b_1, b_{L+1}. 2L+2 vertices, 3L+1 propagators, L loops. Massless internal lines. Value = PhiLadder[3, 1/3, 1/5].

Basso-Dixon fishnet family, basso-dixon_L3x1_massless-internal-offshell-external. Basso-Dixon fishnet B_{M,N}: an M x N rectangular grid, 4 external operators at the corners, defined in dual (region-momentum) space via products of 1/x_ij^2 propagators. The L = MN loop integral is conformal. Momentum space: the (M+1) x (N+1) vertex grid (2MN+M+N massless edges, 4 off-shell corner legs, MN loops); B_{M,1} = the M-rung 4-pt ladder; transpose symmetry fixes the canonical orientation M >= N. Value = BassoDixonI[3, 1, 1/3, 1/5].
